title: Add Decimals by Aligning Place Values
description: Add two decimal numbers by aligning place values and summing corresponding digits.
condition: Expression of the form a + b where a and b are decimal numbers.
response: Align decimal points and add by place value to compute the sum.
label: Principle / Model
notes: [merged: condition varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Apply Order of Operations
description: Evaluate numerical expressions with parentheses and multiple operations in the correct order.
condition: Expression containing parentheses and two or more operations.
response: Evaluate inside parentheses first, then multiplication/division, then addition/subtraction.
label: Principle
notes: Follows conventional order (PEMDAS).

title: Compare Decimal Numbers
description: Determine the relative size of two decimal numbers using place value reasoning.
condition: Two decimal numbers or equivalent expressions are presented with a comparison blank.
response: Insert >, <, or = based on place value comparison.
label: Principle / Model
example: $6.196 > 6.087$ because 196 thousandths is greater than 87 thousandths.
notes: [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Compute Product of Decimals
description: Multiply two decimal numbers by multiplying as whole numbers and placing the decimal point based on total decimal places.
condition: Two decimal numbers are given to multiply (e.g., a × b where a and/or b are decimals).
response: Multiply as whole numbers, count total decimal places in factors, and place decimal in product accordingly.
label: Principle / Model
example: Compute $8.2 \times 0.03$: \(82 \times 3 = 246\). There are 3 decimal places total, so the product is $0.246$.
notes: Core algorithm for decimal multiplication.

title: Compute Remainder with Decimals
description: After finding a whole-number quotient, multiply back and subtract to find the remaining decimal amount.
condition: Given dividend a and divisor b with whole-number quotient q where a ÷ b is not exact.
response: Compute remainder r = a - (b × q).
label: Principle
notes: Used in Part B of Q5.

title: Convert Customary Units
description: Convert between customary units using known conversion factors.
condition: Measurement given in inches and convert to feet, or pounds to ounces.
response: Apply known conversion factor (e.g., 12 in = 1 ft, 16 oz = 1 lb) to compute equivalent measure.
label: Rule / Plan
notes: Conversion factors must be memorized.

title: Convert Decimal to Unit Form
description: Express a decimal as a whole number multiplied by a place-value unit (e.g., tenths, hundredths).
condition: A decimal number is given (e.g., 0.2 or 0.08).
response: State the number in unit form using place value (e.g., 2 tenths, 8 hundredths).
label: Principle / Model
notes: Requires understanding of place value units.

title: Convert Measurement Units
description: Convert a quantity from one unit to another using a known multiplicative relationship.
condition: Measurement given in one unit with a target unit specified (e.g., lb to oz, mL to L).
response: Multiply or divide by the appropriate conversion factor to express the equivalent quantity.
label: Principle
notes: Requires recalling standard conversion constants (e.g., 16 oz = 1 lb).

title: Convert Within Metric System
description: Convert between metric units by multiplying or dividing by powers of 10.
condition: A measurement in metric units (e.g., cm, g, mL) to be converted to another metric unit.
response: Multiply or divide by the appropriate power of 10.
label: Principle
notes: Based on base‑10 structure of metric system.

title: Decompose to Make a Friendly Sum
description: Break apart a number to create a benchmark or whole-number sum that simplifies computation.
condition: Addition expression where one addend can be split to complete a whole with another addend (e.g., 3.77 + 6.84).
response: Decompose one addend (e.g., 6.84 = 0.23 + 6.61) to form an easier partial sum, then combine.
label: Principle
notes: Uses associative and commutative properties.

title: Divide by Powers of 10 Using Place Value
description: Shift the decimal point left when dividing by 10, 100, 1000, etc.
condition: Division where divisor is 10, 100, 1000, or a multiple thereof.
response: Move decimal point left according to the number of zeros in the divisor.
label: Principle
notes: Supports estimation and exact division with powers of 10.

title: Divide Decimal by Whole Number
description: Use standard division to divide a decimal number by a whole number.
condition: Expression of the form a (decimal) ÷ b (whole number).
response: Perform division, placing the decimal point correctly in the quotient.
label: Principle
notes: Includes multi-digit whole divisors.

title: Divide Decimals
description: Compute a quotient involving decimals, adjusting place value as needed.
condition: Division expression with decimal dividend and/or divisor.
response: Scale numbers to remove decimals (if needed) and divide to find the quotient.
label: Principle
notes: Includes dividing remaining total by equal groups in word problems.

title: Equivalent Decimals with Trailing Zeros
description: Recognize that adding or removing trailing zeros to the right of a decimal does not change its value.
condition: A decimal number is written with additional trailing zeros (e.g., 4.02 and 4.020).
response: State that the numbers are equal in value.
label: Concept
example: $4.02 = 4.020$ because the trailing zero in the thousandths place does not change the value.
notes: Relies on understanding of place value and zero as a placeholder.

title: Estimate Decimal Product by Rounding
description: Estimate a product by rounding factors to compatible benchmark numbers and multiplying.
condition: A multiplication expression with decimals is given and an estimate is requested.
response: Round one or both factors to nearby friendly numbers and multiply to produce an approximate product.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Choice of benchmark numbers may vary but should be reasonable.

title: Estimate Decimal Quotient
description: Use compatible numbers or place value reasoning to approximate a decimal division.
condition: Instruction to estimate a quotient involving decimals.
response: Round numbers to compatible values and compute an approximate quotient.
label: Principle
notes: Answers selected from given choices.

title: Estimate Decimal Sum or Difference
description: Estimate a decimal addition or subtraction by rounding each number to the nearest whole number first.
condition: A decimal sum or difference is to be estimated to the nearest whole number.
response: Round each number to the nearest whole number, then compute the resulting sum or difference.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Builds on rounding knowledge component.

title: Evaluate Expression with Parentheses
description: Apply order of operations by evaluating expressions inside parentheses first.
condition: A numerical expression containing parentheses.
response: Compute inside parentheses before performing outside operations.
label: Principle
notes: Order of operations (PEMDAS).

title: Find Fraction of Total Using Multiplication
description: Interpret phrases like '7 tenths of 3.6' as multiplication of a decimal and a whole/decimal number.
condition: A quantity is described as a fraction or decimal part of a total (e.g., 7 tenths of 3.6).
response: Multiply the total by the fractional/decimal amount (e.g., 3.6 × 0.7).
label: Principle / Model

title: Find Whole-Number Quotient from Decimal Division
description: Determine how many whole groups can be made when dividing decimals in a context requiring whole items.
condition: Division a ÷ b in a context where only whole units (e.g., vases) are allowed.
response: Compute quotient and take the greatest whole number less than or equal to the exact result.
label: Principle
example: $28.4 \div 1.3 \approx 21.8$, so Toby can make $21$ whole vases.
notes: Context restricts answer to whole numbers.

title: Identify Decimal Place Value
description: Determine the value of a digit based on its position in a decimal number.
condition: A specific digit in a decimal number is underlined or highlighted.
response: State the digit’s value (e.g., 2 hundredths, 8 thousandths).
label: Principle / Model
example: In $798.228$, the underlined 2 in the hundredths place has value $0.02$ (2 hundredths).
notes: [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Interpret Decimal from Area Model
description: Translate a shaded base-ten area model into a decimal by counting parts out of 1 whole.
condition: Area model representing 1 whole partitioned into tenths, hundredths, or thousandths with some parts shaded.
response: Express shaded parts as a fraction of 1 and convert to a decimal.
label: Principle
example: If $205$ out of $1000$ parts are shaded, then $\frac{205}{1000} = 0.205$.
notes: Requires recognizing total equal parts.

title: Interpret Decimal Place Value Model
description: Translate a visual place value model (ones, tenths, hundredths) into its corresponding decimal expression.
condition: A base-ten or place value model with ones, tenths, and hundredths is shown.
response: Count units in each place and write the corresponding decimal expression.
label: Principle / Model
example: A model showing 1 one, 5 tenths, and 4 hundredths represents $1.54$.
notes: Includes interpreting addition or subtraction shown in the model.

title: Interpret Number Line Subtraction Model
description: Match a number line jump model to the subtraction expression it represents.
condition: A number line shows a jump from one decimal value to another.
response: Identify the starting and ending points and write the corresponding subtraction expression.
label: Principle / Model
example: A jump from $4.5$ back to $3.8$ represents $4.5 - 3.8$.
notes: Requires understanding subtraction as distance on a number line.

title: Interpret Operation Vocabulary
description: Match operation words (sum, difference, product, quotient) to symbols.
condition: A verbal phrase such as 'difference of' or 'quotient of.'
response: Translate to the corresponding operation symbol (+, −, ×, ÷).
label: Concept
notes: Vocabulary-to-symbol mapping.

title: Interpret Thousandths in Area Model
description: Interpret a shaded area model divided into 1000 equal parts as thousandths of a whole.
condition: Area model representing 1 whole partitioned into 1000 equal parts with some shaded.
response: Express shaded parts as a fraction over 1000 and as a decimal.
label: Principle / Model
example: If 91 of 1000 parts are shaded, then $\frac{91}{1000} = 0.091$.

title: Model Decimal Addition on Number Line
description: Represent addition of decimals as movement to the right on a number line.
condition: Expression of the form a + b with a number line provided.
response: Draw an arrow starting at a and ending at a + b.
label: Production / Skill
example: Start at $1.2$ and move right $3.7$ units to land at $4.9$.
notes: [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Model Decimal Multiplication with Area Model
description: Use a 10×10 grid (representing 1 whole) to shade overlapping tenths and hundredths to represent a decimal product.
condition: An area model representing 1 whole is provided and a decimal product (e.g., 0.6 × 0.4) must be represented.
response: Shade rows/columns for each factor and identify overlapping shaded region as the product.
label: Production / Skill
example: Shade 6 tenths one direction and 4 tenths the other; overlap is $24$ hundredths $= 0.24$.
notes: Relies on visual-spatial representation of place value.

title: Multiply by 1000 Shifts Digits
description: Understand that multiplying a decimal by 1,000 shifts each digit three places to the left in a place value chart.
condition: A number is multiplied by 1,000.
response: Move each digit three places to the left (or decimal point three places to the right).
label: Principle / Model
notes: Equivalent to moving decimal point three places right.

title: Place Value is 10 Times Adjacent Left
description: Recognize that each place value is 10 times the value of the place to its right and 1/10 of the place to its left.
condition: Comparing the value of the same digit in two adjacent place values (e.g., hundreds vs tens, hundredths vs tenths).
response: State that the value differs by a factor of 10 (or 1/10) depending on direction.
label: Principle / Model
example: A digit in the hundreds place is $10$ times the value of the same digit in the tens place.
notes: Core base‑ten structure understanding.

title: Represent Repeated Groups with Multiplication
description: Use multiplication to represent equal groups.
condition: A quantity repeated the same number of times (e.g., 4 days of same total hours).
response: Multiply the single-group amount by the number of groups.
label: Principle
notes: Applies to days, bottles, etc.

title: Round to a Specified Place
description: Round a number to a given place value by checking the digit to its right.
condition: A number and a specified place (e.g., nearest tenth, nearest hundredth) are given.
response: If the digit to the right is 5 or more, increase the rounding digit by 1; otherwise keep it the same and truncate remaining digits.
label: Rule / Plan
example: Round $378.089$ to the nearest tenth: hundredths digit is 8, so $378.1$.
notes: Standard rounding rule.

title: Round to Nearest Whole Number
description: Round a decimal number to the nearest whole number based on the tenths digit.
condition: A decimal number must be approximated to the nearest whole number.
response: If the tenths digit is 5 or more, round up; otherwise round down.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Used before estimating sums or differences.

title: Scale Decimals to Divide
description: Multiply dividend and divisor by the same power of 10 to make the divisor a whole number before dividing.
condition: Division problem with a decimal divisor (e.g., a ÷ 0.b or a ÷ 0.bc).
response: Multiply both numbers by 10^n to make divisor whole, then divide.
label: Principle
notes: Also applies to $11.2 \div 0.14$ and $0.55 \div 0.44$.

title: Select Operation from Word Problem Context
description: Determine whether to add or subtract decimals based on a real-world context (e.g., money left, total used).
condition: A word problem describes combining quantities or finding what remains.
response: Choose addition for combining totals; choose subtraction for finding difference or remainder.
label: Concept
example: Tara has $5.40$ and spends $3.67$; compute $5.40 - 3.67$ to find what remains.
notes: Operation choice depends on language cues like 'left' or 'total.'

title: Shade Area Model for Decimal
description: Represent a decimal on a 10×10 (and subdivided) area model by shading tenths, hundredths, and thousandths.
condition: A decimal less than 1 is given and a base‑ten area model is provided.
response: Shade the corresponding number of tenths, hundredths, and thousandths to match the decimal.
label: Production / Skill
example: For $0.365$, shade 3 tenths, 6 hundredths, and 5 thousandths of the whole.
notes: Primarily procedural/visual representation skill.

title: Solve Equal Groups with Unknown Unit Cost
description: Find a unit amount when total cost and some component costs are known.
condition: Word problem with total amount, known partial costs, and equal unknown group costs.
response: Subtract known costs from total, then divide remainder by number of equal groups.
label: Principle
example: If total is $104.16$ and used books sum to $46.65$, then new books total $57.51$ and each costs $57.51 \div 3 = 19.17$.
notes: Multi-step problem combining addition, subtraction, and division.

title: Solve Missing Addend with Decimals
description: Find a missing number in a decimal addition equation.
condition: An equation of the form ___ + a = b or a + ___ = b with decimals is given.
response: Subtract the known addend from the total to find the missing number.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Uses inverse relationship between addition and subtraction.

title: Solve Missing Subtrahend with Decimals
description: Find a missing number in a decimal subtraction equation.
condition: An equation of the form a − ___ = b with decimals is given.
response: Subtract the result from the minuend or solve using inverse addition to find the missing number.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Relies on understanding of inverse operations.

title: Solve Multi-Step Decimal Word Problem
description: Translate a real-world situation into operations with decimals and compute the result.
condition: Word problem involving decimal addition/subtraction and division or multiplication.
response: Form expression, compute total or remaining amount, and divide or multiply as required.
label: Principle / Model

title: Subtract Decimal Numbers
description: Subtract one decimal number from another by aligning place values and regrouping if necessary.
condition: Two decimal numbers are presented with a subtraction operation.
response: Align decimal points and subtract corresponding place values, regrouping as needed.
label: Principle / Model
notes: May require regrouping across place values. [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Subtract Decimals Using Compensation
description: Subtract decimals by adjusting one number to a friendly value and compensating.
condition: Expression of the form a − b where b is adjusted to a nearby friendly number.
response: Subtract the friendly number, then add or subtract the adjustment to compensate.
label: Principle / Model

title: Subtract Fractional Part from Total
description: Subtract a computed fractional part from the total to find the remaining amount.
condition: The total amount and a known fractional part of it are given.
response: Subtract the fractional part from the total.
label: Principle / Model
example: If total is $3.6$ and first day is $2.52$, then second day is $3.6 - 2.52 = 1.08$.

title: Translate Word Problem to Numerical Expression
description: Convert a multi-step word problem into a single numerical expression.
condition: A word problem describing quantities and operations.
response: Write an expression using numbers, operation symbols, and parentheses that models the situation.
label: Rule
notes: Requires identifying structure and grouping.

title: Write Decimal in Expanded Form
description: Express a decimal as the sum of each digit multiplied by its place value unit fraction.
condition: A decimal number is given.
response: Write it as a sum of (digit × place value), e.g., tenths, hundredths, thousandths.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Requires identifying each digit’s place value.
